name: Transnevada Ruta H — De Capileira al Refugio Poqueira — paseo por el Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada
region_intro: Hiking around Capileira offers access to the dramatic Barranco de Poqueira gorge, an official Historic-Artistic site within the Sierra Nevada National Park. The region is characterized by its elevated mountainous landscape, featuring high peaks like Veleta and Mulhacén, the highest in mainland Spain. Hikers can experience diverse terrain, from rocky high-mountain environments to lush river valleys and glacial lakes. Best hiking trails around Capileira The most popular hiking route is Las Cebadillas and Central Hidraúlica de Poqueira – circular from Capileira, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 47 minutes to complete, offering views of the Poqueira gorge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hoya del Portillo and Loma del Cascajar Negro Loop, a moderate 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the surrounding Sierra Nevada landscape. Local hikers also love the Mulhacén Ascent Trail, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail leading through high-mountain terrain towards Spain's highest peak, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Capileira is defined by the Barranco de Poqueira gorge, high-mountain environments, and traditional Alpujarran architecture. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Capileira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 750 reviews. More than 3,400 hikers have used komoot to explore Capileira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Capileira?
A: Capileira offers over 50 hiking trails, catering to all levels from easy walks to challenging high-mountain ascents within the Sierra Nevada National Park. There are 6 easy routes, 19 moderate, and 26 difficult trails to explore.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Capileira?
A: Yes, Capileira has several easy trails, with 6 routes specifically categorized as easy. A good option for families is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980639286/hoya-del-portillo-and-loma-del-cascajar-negro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya del Portillo and Loma del Cascajar Negro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views in a moderate, family-friendly setting.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes near Capileira?
A: For experienced hikers, Capileira serves as a gateway to high-mountain routes, including ascents to Spain's highest peaks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984019771/mulhacen-ascent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mulhacén Ascent Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 10.9-mile route leading through high-mountain terrain towards Mulhacén, often taking over 7 hours. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977966863/mulhacen-peak-loop-from-refugio-poqueira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mulhacén Peak Loop from Refugio Poqueira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes starting from Capileira?
A: Yes, many routes around Capileira are circular. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979021401/las-cebadillas-and-central-hidraulica-de-poqueira-circular-from-capileira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cebadillas and Central Hidraúlica de Poqueira – circular from Capileira&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.4-mile trail offering views of the dramatic Poqueira gorge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980644443/parque-nacional-de-sierra-nevada-circular-to-hoya-del-portillo-from-capileira" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada – circular to Hoya del Portillo from Capileira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on hikes around Capileira?
A: Hikes around Capileira offer breathtaking views of the Barranco de Poqueira gorge and the Sierra Nevada. You can find stunning vistas from points like Tajo del Diablo and Tajo del Perchel. The region also features diverse terrain, from rocky high-mountain environments to lush river valleys, and on clear days, you might even glimpse the Mediterranean Sea.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes to discover on trails near Capileira?
A: Yes, the area around Capileira features beautiful natural water formations. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tajo de Cortés Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, high-mountain routes may lead to glacial lakes like Laguna de la Caldera and Río Seco, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is also nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Capileira?
A: The best time for hiking in Capileira generally depends on the desired experience. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer is ideal for high-altitude routes, while winter provides opportunities for snowshoeing or winter mountaineering, though some higher trails may be inaccessible due to snow.

Q: Is parking available for hikers in Capileira?
A: Yes, parking is available. For those heading to higher elevations, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4353960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de San Juan Parking – Ascent to Mulhacén&lt;/a&gt; is a key starting point for routes towards Mulhacén.

Q: Can I reach hiking trailheads near Capileira using public transport?
A: Capileira itself is accessible by public transport, and from the village, many trailheads are within walking distance. For specific high-mountain access points like Hoya del Portillo, seasonal bus services often operate from Capileira, especially during peak hiking seasons, to reduce private vehicle traffic in the National Park.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Capileira?
A: The hiking routes in Capileira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 750 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views of the Sierra Nevada, the dramatic Barranco de Poqueira gorge, and the unique experience of exploring traditional Alpujarran architecture within the white villages.

Q: Are there any traditional villages or cultural sights to see along the trails?
A: Capileira itself is a beautiful traditional white village, and many trails, such as those through the Poqueira Gorge, connect it with other picturesque villages like Pampaneira and Bubión. These routes offer a chance to appreciate the unique Alpujarran architecture and ancient irrigation channels known as *acequias*.

Q: Are there any huts or refuges available for longer hikes?
A: Yes, for longer high-mountain excursions, there are huts and refuges. The refuge at Las Eras de Aldeire (around 2,500 meters above sea level) is a key point for those undertaking routes to Mulhacén and Veleta. Other options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carihuela Bivouac Shelter&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4473243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Hostel&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: The small town of Capileira, in addition to being beautiful, is surrounded by trails to walk through to enjoy nature. Its architecture is typical of La Alpujarra: narrow, steep streets, flanked by houses with their traditional chimneys and "terraos" (the typical roofs of the region).
Good place to bivouac or camp one night under the stars
This pine forest is the highest in Europe around 3000m above sea level.
Here we have some Corrales in the port of Molina, the truth is that if we were around here in the middle of a snowstorm and clothing it would be a good place to take refuge

Maintained hut and perfect overnight spot for a multi-day tour over the Sierra Nevada. Attention: Food is available punctually at 8 p.m. (sometimes earlier in the darker season), and the hut can be booked out quickly in the summer months. In autumn or spring, however, it is rather tranquil. Tip: To be on the safe side, ask the Sierra Nevada Info-Line about the current opening times before the tour. You can find the telephone number here: http://www.granadadirect.com/provincia/telefonos-sierra-nevada/
Scots pine forest on Loma de Piedra Blanca, between Hoya del Portillo and Prado de las Juncias.

La Hoya del Portillo, located at an altitude of 2,150 meters, is located at the end of the public road that arrives from Capileira. In the recreational area you can leave your car and go to the Altas Cumbres Interpretation Center to find out about the Sierra Nevada National Park.
